title: hydrangea hortensia var. caerulea
intro: Hydrangea hortensia var. caerulea, also known as the blue hydrangea, is a flowering plant species within the Hydrangea genus. It is primarily used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as alleviating arthritis and gout symptoms, as well as treating fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. Hydrangea hortensia var. caerulea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
